business.title: World’s first quantum battery could enable ultra fast charging
business.description: Scientists in Australia have demonstrated a prototype quantum battery that could revolutionize energy storage. By harnessing quantum effects, it can absorb energy in a rapid “super absorption” event, enabling much faster charging than conventional batteries. Even more surprisingly, the system becomes more efficient as it scales up. The research opens the door to ultra-fast, next-generation energy technologies.

business.title: Nikkei sinks over 5% after Trump threatens Iran over Strait of Hormuz
business.description: The Nikkei stock index briefly sank over 5 percent Monday morning, as crude oil futures surged after U.S. President Donald Trump warned that power plants would be attacked if Iran does not lift the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

science.title: Baby dinosaur discovered in South Korea
science.description: Scientists have used X-rays to reveal a young dinosaur skeleton in South Korea. The dinosaur has been named Doolysaurus after a popular children's cartoon.

science.title: How humans came to inhabit every corner of the world
science.description: A new study reveals how cultural evolution, not biology, enabled humans to inhabit diverse environments across the globe.

science.title: Scientists twisted a mysterious superconductor and got a shocking result
science.description: A decades-old superconducting mystery just took a surprising turn. Strontium ruthenate, a material that conducts electricity with zero resistance at low temperatures, has long puzzled scientists with hints of an exotic, complex superconducting state. But by carefully twisting and distorting ultra-thin crystals, researchers found something unexpected: the material barely reacted at all. This challenges years of assumptions and suggests its behavior may be far simpler—or far stranger—than previously thought.

science.title: Sun was part of mass migration of stars from Galaxy Core
science.description: The Sun originated about 10,000 lightyears closer to the core of the Milky Way galaxy than its present location. Scientists have determined that the Sun was part of an exodus of stars from the central regions of the galaxy.

science.title: This floating time crystal breaks Newton’s third law of motion
science.description: Scientists have created a new kind of time crystal using sound waves to levitate tiny beads in mid-air. These particles interact in a one-sided, unbalanced way, breaking the usual rules of motion and creating a steady, repeating rhythm. The system is surprisingly simple yet reveals complex physics with big implications. It could help advance quantum computing and deepen our understanding of biological timing systems.

Meta is going all-in on AI with the company likely planning a major round of layoffs amid soaring costs.
